data.title: Iran's Oil Sales Remain Strong Amid Military Conflict
data.description:
Iran's Oil Minister Mohsen Paknejad announced on April 14 that the country's oil sales have been robust since the onset of military conflicts with the United States and Israel. According to BlockBeats, a portion of the revenue generated will be allocated to repairing industrial damage caused by wartime attacks.

Paknejad highlighted that Iranian oil industry workers have maintained operations at various facilities throughout the conflict, ensuring that oil exports have not been interrupted, even for a single day. This includes key export hubs such as Kharg Island.

data.title: Trump's AI-Generated Image Sparks Backlash from Religious Right
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump has sparked controversy by releasing an AI-generated image depicting himself as Jesus Christ. According to BlockBeats, this move has angered the religious right, marking the strongest backlash from Catholic and evangelical supporters since his return to the White House.

John Yep, CEO of the nonprofit organization 'Catholics for Catholics,' expressed confusion over the incident. He noted that Catholics have overwhelmingly supported Trump, and this act of disrespect towards their faith has left them bewildered. The organization has previously hosted religious events at Trump's Mar-a-Lago estate and maintained close ties with his administration.

Despite initial skepticism, conservative Christians have consistently backed Trump through two impeachments and three elections. Trump has fulfilled campaign promises to them, including nominating Supreme Court justices who helped overturn the Roe v. Wade decision. However, for many Christian leaders, the image posted by Trump on Truth Social, along with his sharp criticism of Pope Leo XIV, has crossed a line.

data.title: PA Beacon Launches to Enhance Polymarket Insights
data.description:
PA Beacon, a market prediction tracking tool, has officially launched, offering smart money observation and real-time alerts for Polymarket. According to PANews, PA Beacon organizes decentralized prediction market fund activities into a continuous signal stream through candidate pool screening, address profiling, leaderboard generation, and minute-level monitoring. It is now in public operation.

PA Beacon currently features four smart money leaderboards: Small Strong List, Steady Profit List, Active Alpha List, and High Win Rate List, which update every six hours. Additionally, it operates two minute-level monitoring lines, including a Watchlist for key addresses and a 'Large Radar' for transactions over $20,000, covering sectors such as sports, politics, geopolitics, and esports.

data.title: Swedish Central Bank Deputy Governor Discusses Inflation Impact
data.description: Swedish Central Bank Deputy Governor Bunge stated that the impact on inflation depends on the duration of the war, the speed of production recovery in the region, and the response of businesses and households to the situation. According to Jin10, these factors will play a crucial role in determining the inflationary pressures faced by the economy.

data.title: Sen. Tillis to Release Draft Clarity Act Language on Stablecoin Yield
data.description: Senator Thom Tillis announced plans to unveil draft language for the Clarity Act this week, aiming to clarify whether cryptocurrency firms can offer yield on idle stablecoin balances. According to NS3.AI, the proposal has been reviewed by representatives from both the banking and cryptocurrency sectors, but has faced criticism from banks, though the specific source of the pushback remains undisclosed. The bill is required to pass through the Senate Banking Committee and be reconciled with the Senate Agriculture Committee before it can proceed to a full floor vote.

data.title: Iran claims oil exports have not been interrupted for a single day.
data.description: Iranian Oil Minister Mohsen Paknead said today (April 14) that Iran's oil sales have been good since the outbreak of the military conflict with the United States and Israel, and part of the revenue will be used to repair the damage to industry caused by the wartime attacks. Paknead said that Iranian oil industry workers have maintained the operation of various facilities during the conflict, ensuring that oil exports "have not been interrupted for even a day," including key export hubs such as Kharg Island. (CCTV International News)

data.title: Shanghai Aunt Shares Surge Over 11% on Hong Kong Stock Exchange
data.description: Shares of Shanghai Aunt (02589.HK) experienced a significant increase, rising over 11% on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. According to Jin10, the stock saw a sharp upward movement, attracting attention from investors. The reasons behind this surge remain under analysis as market participants assess the factors contributing to the stock's performance.

data.title: Sweden's Central Bank Vice Governor Commits to Low Inflation and Financial Stability
data.description: Sweden's Central Bank Vice Governor, Per Jansson, emphasized the institution's commitment to maintaining low and stable inflation, a stable financial system, and the ability to facilitate payments during crises and wartime. According to Jin10, Jansson highlighted the importance of these objectives in ensuring economic resilience and stability. The central bank's focus remains on safeguarding the financial system while addressing challenges posed by global uncertainties.

data.title: Nasdaq to Include SanDisk in Nasdaq 100 Index by 2026
data.description:
On April 14, Nasdaq announced that SanDisk (SNDK) will be officially included in the Nasdaq 100 Index components before the market opens on April 20, 2026. According to BlockBeats, this announcement has significantly boosted SNDK's stock performance, with the price rising over 14% since Monday, reaching a new all-time high. On the Hyperliquid platform, SNDK contracts surged above $980, marking a 24-hour increase of 13.8%.

Hyperinsight monitoring indicates that the strengthening market has turned on-chain long positions profitable. Notably, a trader known as "CBB," who previously focused on shorting U.S. stocks, shifted to long positions in SNDK starting April 6. This has become their primary holding, with a current position size of $6.73 million and an unrealized profit of $730,000, making them the largest long position holder in SNDK on Hyperliquid.

data.title: X's Head of Product Suggests Launch to Address Crypto Challenges
data.description: Nikita Bier, X's head of product, recently commented on the challenging year for cryptocurrency, proposing the launch of a solution to address these issues. According to NS3.AI, Bier's remarks quickly gained attention, amassing over 677,000 views within hours. Meanwhile, Elon Musk announced last month that X Money is set to launch in April, featuring peer-to-peer transfers, bank deposits, a debit card, and cashback rewards. However, the public details currently describe the product as fiat-based, with no confirmed integration of cryptocurrency functionality.

data.title: Nomura: Rising oil prices may delay the Bank of Japan's interest rate hike.
data.description: Nomura economists stated that Japan faces "unprecedented risks" ahead due to persistently rising crude oil prices and concerns about petroleum product supplies, making it highly likely that the Bank of Japan will postpone interest rate hikes. Compared to his March press conference, Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda expressed greater concern in his Monday remarks about the potential downside risks to the economy from the Middle East conflict. Ueda stated that if the conflict continues, it will impact the business activities of Japanese companies through supply chain disruptions. (Jinshi)

data.title: Volatile Altcoin Surges Raise Concerns Among Investors
data.description:
On April 14, several altcoins, notably RAVE, experienced significant surges driven by market manipulation, according to BlockBeats. Despite the controversial nature of these deliberate price hikes, many aggressive investors are participating in hopes of finding the next big opportunity.

Amid this speculative environment, some project teams are exploiting the situation to quickly elevate and offload their tokens. They often create a false impression of a 'monster coin' by generating a large hourly candlestick, prompting retail investors to buy in, only to rapidly sell off their holdings, causing the price to plummet. FF and INX are notable examples of this practice.

FF saw a rapid increase in the early hours of April 11, doubling its price from $0.07 within an hour and reaching a peak of $0.17641, before quickly declining. As of the report, the token has fallen back to $0.07786.

The INX team openly engaged in similar tactics, doubling the token's price in a day before at least two addresses sold off unlocked tokens worth approximately $400,000, leading to a sharp price drop.

It is noteworthy that both FF and INX were once high-profile projects with substantial funding, securing $20 million and $65.3 million, respectively. Their current reliance on such tactics to regain liquidity is a cause for concern.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Novo Nordisk Partners with OpenAI to Enhance Employee Skills
data.description: Novo Nordisk has announced a collaboration with OpenAI aimed at enhancing the skills of its global workforce and boosting their proficiency in artificial intelligence. According to Jin10, this partnership is part of Novo Nordisk's broader strategy to integrate advanced AI capabilities into its operations, ensuring that employees are well-equipped to leverage these technologies effectively. The initiative underscores the growing importance of AI in the pharmaceutical industry, as companies seek to harness its potential to drive innovation and improve efficiency.

data.title: The U.S. Department of Justice has initiated compensation proceedings for OneCoin victims, with over $40 million in funds available for distribution.
data.description: The U.S. Department of Justice announced the initiation of restitution proceedings for victims of the OneCoin cryptocurrency fraud, with over $40 million in forfeited assets available for compensation. The program targets investors who purchased OneCoin between 2014 and 2019 and incurred net losses. U.S. prosecutors stated that this move aims to return a portion of the illicit proceeds to the victims. OneCoin was launched by Ruja Ignatova and Karl Greenwood, and the related fraud exceeded $4 billion. Ignatova disappeared in 2017, and Greenwood was sentenced to 20 years in prison in 2023. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Anthropic has hired Ballard Partners to lobby after negotiations with the Pentagon broke down over restrictions on the use of AI.
data.description: Anthropic has hired Ballard Partners, a lobbying firm with close ties to the Trump administration. Disclosure documents show the partnership took effect on March 9, close to the Pentagon's assessment of its supply chain risks. Reports indicate that the core disagreement in previous negotiations stemmed from the scope of AI use. The Pentagon demanded unrestricted use of its tools, while Anthropic sought restrictions preventing its products from being used for fully autonomous weapons deployments or mass surveillance of U.S. citizens. Furthermore, Ballard Partners is the sixth lobbying firm Anthropic has hired since November 2024. Data shows its federal lobbying spending in 2025 is projected to increase by over 330% year-over-year to approximately $3.1 million, reflecting a continued rise in investment in AI industry policy communication. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $291 million yesterday, with Fidelity's FBTC experiencing the largest net outflow at $229 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $291 million yesterday (April 13th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was BlackRock ETF IBIT, with a net inflow of $34.7012 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $63.762 billion. This was followed by Bitwise ETF BITB, with a net inflow of $11.8758 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.102 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was Fidelity ETF FBTC, with a net outflow of $229 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $10.883 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $94.508 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.45%, and a cumulative net inflow of $56.453 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $9.4404 million yesterday, marking the third consecutive day of net inflows.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $9.4404 million yesterday (April 13th, Eastern Time). The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the BlackRock Stalled ETH ETF (ETHB), with a net inflow of $5.7776 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $383 million. This was followed by the Grayscale Ethereum Mini Trust ETF (ETH), with a net inflow of $5.1508 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $1.855 billion. The Ethereum spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (ETHA), with a net outflow of $4.0704 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $11.727 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of the Ethereum spot ETF is $12.975 billion, the ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) is 4.77%, and the historical cumulative net inflow has reached $11.677 billion.

data.title: Western Australia to Establish Strategic Diesel Reserves Amid Shortages
data.description: Western Australia is set to create its own strategic reserves of diesel fuel in response to acute shortages affecting the region. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the state's proactive measures to ensure energy security and stability. The decision comes as part of a broader strategy to mitigate the impact of supply disruptions and maintain essential services. The establishment of these reserves aims to provide a buffer against future shortages and support the local economy. Authorities are working on the logistics and infrastructure required to implement this plan effectively. This move underscores the importance of energy resilience in the face of global supply chain challenges.

data.title: Japan Faces Unprecedented Risks Amid Rising Oil Prices, Nomura Economist Says
data.description: Nomura economists have highlighted Japan's exposure to unprecedented risks due to the continuous rise in oil prices and concerns over the supply of petroleum products. According to Jin10, the Bank of Japan is likely to delay interest rate hikes in response to these challenges. In a speech on Monday, Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda expressed increased concern over the potential economic downturn risks posed by Middle Eastern conflicts, compared to his remarks at the March press conference. Ueda noted that if the conflict persists, it could disrupt supply chains and adversely affect the operations of Japanese businesses.

data.title: Aptos Announces Tokenomics Update with Key Adjustments
data.description: On April 14, Aptos released an update to its tokenomics. According to BlockBeats, the main changes include a reduction in the annualized staking rewards rate from 5.19% to 2.6% and a tenfold increase in gas fees, although stablecoin transfer costs remain low at approximately $0.00014. The launch of Decibel DEX is expected to significantly boost on-chain transaction volume and gas fee burning, with an estimated annual burn of over 32 million APT. A protocol-level hard cap has been set for the total supply at 2.1 billion APT. Additionally, the Aptos Foundation will permanently lock and stake 210 million APT. Future incentives will be triggered by milestones, and there is exploration of a programmatic buyback plan.
